post.description:
Start a reset: email a single use link to a known active account.

Always returns 204 regardless of whether the email matches an account, so the endpoint cannot be
used to enumerate users. When the email does match, a token row is stored (only its hash) and a
link is sent. With SMTP unconfigured the mailer logs the link instead, so dev still works.

Finish a reset: spend the token and set the new password.

The token is matched by hash. A missing, already used, or expired token returns the same 400 so
a caller cannot tell which. On success the password is updated, an invited account is flipped to
active, and the token is marked used so the link cannot be replayed.
Enrich a short capture into a fuller description. Used by Generate with AI.

parameters.name: topic_id

schema.description: filter to entries under this topic
schema.title: Topic Id

parameters.description: filter to entries under this topic

schema.title: Response List Entries Journal Entries Get
Response List Topics Journal Topics Get
Response List Attachments Journal Entries  Entry Id  Attachments Get

post.description:
Transcribe a photo of a handwritten page via the vision model, returning the text.

The image is processed in memory and never written to disk, consistent with the journal image
rule. The returned text becomes an entry body or an attachment note, at the client's choice.
When no provider key backs the vision model the route returns 501, never a stub.

Accept a journal entry from an external source, authenticated by a per source token.

This route is machine to machine, not session authenticated: the per source token is the
credential. The token is validated against the server side token map, then discarded; it never
enters the entry. A malformed payload is rejected by the schema; an unknown source or a wrong
token is a 401. The created entry is tagged with its source so the origin stays traceable.

schema.title: Response List Flow Items Flow Items Get

get.description:
Stream the project's on disk folder as a zip the browser can download.

Zips every file under NEXA_PROJECTS_ROOT/<slug> through the path safety gate, so the user
gets the real project directory (project_plan.md and the rest, unzipping under a <slug>
folder). Returns 404 when the project has not been processed yet and the folder is absent.

post.description:
Run the readiness assessment for this item's project at the Human Gate.

Answers every item it can from the knowledge sources before asking the user; blocking gaps land
in the existing approval queue and credential gaps in the secure provide path. A secret is never
written here. Returns the fresh assessment.

get.description:
The latest readiness assessment for this item's project.

When the project has never been assessed, return an unassessed result (run_id 0, not
satisfied, no items) rather than a 404. The gate stays closed because satisfied is false, and
the panel shows an honest not yet assessed state without a console error.

schema.title: Response List Modes Projects Modes Get

post.description:
Edit a project's builder fields and record the edit as an agent run.

The plan is rewritten through the path safety gate via the project editor, so the change is a
recoverable BuildLogEntry, and the whole edit is captured as an AgentRun with one verified
edit step. Soft and recoverable: nothing is destroyed.

schema.title: Response List Research Projects Research Projects Get

post.description: Create a build project from a research project and attach the research to it in one step.

post.description:
Resolve a waiting_approval step. The single human gate write into the runtime.

Delegates to the resolve_approval writer, which owns only the approval exit edges (approve
moves to planned, reject to skipped). Resolving anything not at the gate is a conflict.

Response Step Status Counts Runtime Runs  Run Id  Status Counts Get
Response List Candidates Dreaming Candidates Get
Response List Runs Dreaming Runs Get

get.description: Every known provider plus any extra provider already connected, with status and hint.

schema.title: Response List Providers Settings Providers Get

post.description:
Connect a provider: write the key to the secret store by reference, keep a last four hint.

The key is written only to the server side store. The row carries the reference and the hint,
never the value, and the response is the non secret status view.

Disconnect a provider: clear the stored key and the row reference.

The stored secret is removed and the row returns to available. A provider still configured
through the server side .env reports connected by environment; this never touches .env.
Pull live models for a connected provider and cache them, auto enabling referenced models.

schema.title: Response Refresh Models Settings Providers  Provider  Refresh Post
Response List Discovered Models Settings Providers Models Get

post.description:
Directly create an active user with a password set, ready to sign in.

If a soft removed user already holds the email, reactivate that row in place rather than
colliding on the unique email index, keeping their items and projects attached.

Provide the secret for a pending credential request.

The secret arrives over the authenticated session only, is written straight to the Brain
secret store, flips the Integration to connected (by reference), and resolves the readiness
step. The response is the integration read model, which carries the reference and never the
value. The secret is never returned, logged, or written to the ledger.

parameters.name: project_id

schema.description: filter to tasks under this project
schema.title: Project Id

parameters.description: filter to tasks under this project

get.description: Liveness probe used by CI and the deploy verification step.

ApprovalRequest.title: ApprovalRequest
ApprovalRequest.description:
A waiting_approval step plus its gate guidance.

Every approval request carries a recommended_default (proceed or change) and a one line
framing, so a human sees a clear default and whether the decision materially affects the
outcome.

CaptureResponse.title: CaptureResponse
CaptureResponse.description: The transcribed text of a handwritten page, plus the vision model that produced it.

ConnectProviderRequest.title: ConnectProviderRequest
ConnectProviderRequest.description:
Connect a model provider by supplying its API key once.

The key is accepted only over the authenticated session and is written straight to the Brain
secret store. It is never echoed back, logged, or written to the ledger.

FulfilCredentialRequest.title: FulfilCredentialRequest
FulfilCredentialRequest.description:
Provide a secret for a pending credential request, by waiting_approval step id.

The secret is accepted only over the authenticated session and is written straight to the
Brain secret store. It is never echoed back, logged, or written to the runtime ledger.

GeneralSettings.title: GeneralSettings
GeneralSettings.description:
Workspace level general settings, the defaults that frame the other surfaces.

general_instructions is the system level instruction prepended to model work. The rest are
presentation and locale preferences.

IngestRequest.title: IngestRequest
IngestRequest.description:
An inbound journal entry from an external source, authenticated by a per source token.

The token authenticates the source and is never stored on the entry. The body becomes the
entry text; the source is recorded as a tag so the origin is traceable.

InsightsResponse.title: InsightsResponse
InsightsResponse.description: The cached latest batch, grouped by category for the four surfaces.

KnowledgeEntryUpdate.title: KnowledgeEntryUpdate
KnowledgeEntryUpdate.description: Partial update. Only the supplied fields change. Archiving has its own endpoint.

KnowledgePolicy.title: KnowledgePolicy
KnowledgePolicy.description:
What the system may ingest, and what is allowed into long term memory.

The memory gate stays human by default: require_approval is on and connector memory is
off, so nothing reaches the Knowledge base without an explicit accept in the Dreaming
review queue.

ProfileUpdate.title: ProfileUpdate
ProfileUpdate.description: Self profile edit from Settings, General.

ProofOfWork.title: ProofOfWork
ProofOfWork.description: The evidence behind one step, and whether that evidence earned a verified status.

ReadinessAssessment.title: ReadinessAssessment
ReadinessAssessment.description: A project's build readiness: every item, whether it is satisfied, and what still blocks.

ReadinessItem.title: ReadinessItem
ReadinessItem.description:
One readiness item: a need the plan declared and how the assessment resolved it.

provider and integration_id are present only for a credential item, so the web can open the
provide control. Neither is or carries a secret value.

ResolveApprovalRequest.title: ResolveApprovalRequest
ResolveApprovalRequest.description:
The one human gate write: approve or reject a waiting_approval step, with an optional note.

The runtime is read only otherwise; this resolves only the approval exit edge through the
resolve_approval writer, never any other field.

UserCreate.title: UserCreate
UserCreate.description:
Directly provision an active, ready to log in user with a password set.

This is the owner path for adding people without an email invite round trip. The created
user is active immediately, so they can sign in with the supplied password.

UserUpdate.title: UserUpdate
UserUpdate.description:
Change a user's role, display name, status, or password. Only supplied fields change.

Setting a password on an invited user makes the account usable, so the server also flips
such a user to active when a password is provided.

WorkspaceUpdate.title: WorkspaceUpdate
WorkspaceUpdate.description: Editable overview fields. Only provided fields are written.
